notes: Display-only presentation pass across Budget, Journal and Net Worth. (1) Expense-split donut (Budget): all EMIs now collapse into a single 'EMIs (combined)' slice instead of one slice per EMI, and the whole chart uses a distinct readable palette with card-colored slice borders. Root cause of the prior black wedges was that Chart.js was being handed raw CSS var() strings (e.g. var(--accent)) which it cannot parse and rendered as black; a shared resolveCssColor + paletteColors helper now fixes this everywhere. (2) Journal adjustments ('Adjustments - balance reconciliations'): the list is now filtered to the CURRENT month only (by date YYYY-MM prefix); past-month adjustments no longer pile up on this view, and the empty state reads 'No adjustments this month.' (3) Net Worth KPIs: the 'Compounding Growth' card (not useful here) is removed and replaced by two cards - 'Non-Liquid Assets' (illiquid investment balances at the chosen month; cash stays under Liquidity) and 'Debts' at the chosen month. Debts = outstanding loan balances (liability accounts, stored negative) + remaining scheduled EMI payments from the chosen month to each EMI's end, respecting step-up/pause/close timeline events. EMIs are separate from liability accounts in the model, so this does not double-count; it counts total installments still due (includes future interest, not principal-only). The KPI row widened from 3 to 4 columns (2x2 on mobile). (4) Net Worth charts: the 'Net Worth Over Time' area fill and line were rendering black for the same unparsed-var() reason and now render in the accent green; the 'Composition on Chosen Date' donut now uses the distinct palette so no slice renders black. Scope is strictly display: no change to projected balances, net worth values, the shared savings chain (projectSavingsChain) or any engine math. KNOWN ITEM (still deliberately deferred to its own careful build): the current-month savings forecast uses the engine's PLANNED surplus, so logging a large current-month expense does not yet re-forecast future savings (next month's plan does reflect it). That change touches projectSavingsChain and needs dedicated double-count verification before it is made. Reusable SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid for re-running all invariants from the console.
